reasoning: The Lean formalization faithfully captures the informal statement. (1) "Quadratic Q" is correctly encoded as Q.natDegree = 2. (2) The identity P = Q·P'' is expressed pointwise over ℂ, which is equivalent to polynomial equality since ℂ is infinite; `Polynomial.derivative^[2] P` correctly denotes the second derivative. (3) "At least two distinct roots" is directly encoded. (4) "n distinct roots" where n = deg P is encoded as an injection from Fin (P.natDegree) into the roots of P — since a degree-n polynomial has at most n distinct roots, this is equivalent to having exactly n distinct roots. Edge cases (P = 0, or deg P ≤ 1) either trivialize the statement or are ruled out by the hypothesis P = Q·P'' itself, so no extra hypotheses are needed.
